loot:
Loot Command

Usage: loot [corpse name]

Loot gold and items from a corpse in the room. If no name is given,
loots the first corpse found. Empty corpses are automatically removed.

Examples:
  loot            - Loot the first corpse
  loot goblin     - Loot the corpse of a goblin

See also: help corpses, help kill
corpses:
Corpses and Looting

When a mob is killed, its body remains as a corpse containing gold and
possibly items. Use the 'loot' command to collect the contents.

Corpses decay after 5 minutes. When a corpse decays, any remaining
items fall to the ground where they can be picked up with 'get'.

Loot quality scales with the mob's Challenge Rating (CR):
  CR 0-1:   Small gold, 5% chance of mundane item
  CR 2-4:   Moderate gold, 10% chance of mundane item
  CR 5-10:  Good gold, 20% chance of minor magic item
  CR 11-16: Large gold, 30% chance of medium magic item
  CR 17+:   Huge gold, 50% chance of major magic item

See also: help loot, help death
buy:
Buy Command

Usage: buy <item name>

Purchase an item from a shopkeeper in the room. Use 'list' to see
available items and prices. You must have enough gold to buy.

The shop automatically restocks when its inventory runs out.

Examples:
  buy dagger
  buy leather armor

See also: help sell, help list, help appraise, help shops
sell:
Sell Command

Usage: sell <item name>

Sell an item from your inventory to a shopkeeper in the room.
The shop pays a percentage of the item's base value (varies by shop).

Examples:
  sell dagger
  sell padded armor

See also: help buy, help list, help appraise, help shops
list:
List / Shop Command

Usage: list
       shop

Show all items for sale from the shopkeeper in the room, including prices.

See also: help buy, help sell, help appraise, help shops
shop:
List / Shop Command

Usage: list
       shop

Show all items for sale from the shopkeeper in the room, including prices.

See also: help buy, help sell, help appraise, help shops
appraise:
Appraise Command

Usage: appraise <item name>

Ask the shopkeeper to appraise an item. Works for items in your
inventory (shows what the shop would pay) or items in the shop's
stock (shows the purchase price).

Examples:
  appraise dagger
  appraise leather armor

See also: help buy, help sell, help shops
shops:
Shop System

Shopkeepers are special NPCs that buy and sell goods. To trade, you
must be in the same room as a shopkeeper.

Commands:
  list / shop         - See what's for sale
  buy <item>          - Buy an item
  sell <item>         - Sell an item from inventory
  appraise <item>     - Check an item's value

Shop prices:
  Buy price = item value x shop's sell rate (often 120%)
  Sell price = item value x shop's buy rate (often 50%)

Shops automatically restock when their inventory runs empty.

See also: help buy, help sell, help gold
give:
Give Command

Usage: give <item> <target>
       give <amount> gold <target>

Give an item or gold to another player or NPC in the room.
Item giving can trigger quest objectives (DELIVER type).

Examples:
  give dagger Mira
  give 50 gold Aelthara

See also: help gold, help inventory
gold:
Gold and Economy

Gold is the primary currency in Oreka. You earn gold by:
  - Looting corpses after killing mobs
  - Selling items to shopkeepers
  - Receiving gold from other players

You spend gold by:
  - Buying items from shopkeepers
  - Giving gold to other players

New characters start with 100 gp.
Use 'score' to see your current gold.

See also: help buy, help sell, help loot, help give
flee:
Flee Command

Usage: flee

Attempt to escape from combat. Base 50% chance, modified by Dex.
On success, you move to a random available exit and leave combat.
On failure, you remain in combat and lose your turn.

See also: help combat
queue:
Queue Command

Usage: queue <type> <action> [args]
       q <type> <action> [args]

Queue an action to replace your next auto-attack in combat.
Supported types: spell, maneuver, skill, feat, item

Examples:
  queue spell magic missile goblin
  queue maneuver disarm goblin
  queue spell cure light wounds

Related:
  showqueue (sq)    - Show your queued action
  clearqueue (cq)   - Cancel your queued action

See also: help combat, help autoattack
autoattack:
Auto-Attack Command

Usage: autoattack [on|off]
       aa [on|off]

Toggle automatic attacks in combat. When enabled (default), your
character attacks their target each combat round. When disabled,
you must queue actions manually.

See also: help queue, help combat, help target
target:
Target Command

Usage: target <name>

Set your combat target without initiating an attack. Useful for
changing targets mid-combat.

See also: help kill, help combat
respawn:
Respawn Command

Usage: respawn

When you are dead (HP <= -10) or dying (HP < 0), use this command
to return to the Chapel of the Aetherial Altar. On respawn:
  - You appear at the Central Aetherial Altar (room 1000)
  - HP restored to 50% of maximum
  - All conditions cleared
  - Combat state cleared
  - XP penalty: 10% of current XP (or 50 XP minimum)

See also: help death, help rest
death:
Death and Dying (D&D 3.5 Rules)

HP Thresholds:
  HP > 0:      Healthy - can act normally
  HP = 0:      Disabled - can take only a single action per round
  HP -1 to -9: Dying - unconscious, lose 1 HP per round
  HP <= -10:   Dead

Stabilization:
  While dying, each round you make a 10% stabilization check.
  If stabilized, you stop losing HP but remain unconscious.
  Other players can stabilize you with healing spells.

Respawn:
  Use 'respawn' when dead or dying to return to the chapel.
  Costs 10% of your current XP.

See also: help respawn, help rest, help conditions
rest:
Rest Command

Usage: rest
       sleep

Rest to recover HP and spell slots. Must be in a safe area
(rooms flagged safe, inn, or temple) and not in combat.

Long Rest (8 hours):
  - Full HP recovery
  - All spell slots restored
  - Clears fatigued and shaken conditions
  - Stabilizes dying characters

Short Rest (1 hour):
  - Recover level + CON modifier HP

See also: help death, help conditions
equipment:
Equipment Commands

Usage: wear <item>       - Equip an item from inventory
       equip <item>      - Same as wear
       remove <item>     - Unequip an item
       unequip <item>    - Same as remove
       equipment / eq    - Show all equipped items

Equipment Slots (D&D 3.5):
  head, neck, shoulders, body, torso, waist, arms,
  hands, main_hand, off_hand, ring1, ring2, feet, back

Equipped armor affects your AC. Heavier armor limits your
Dex bonus to AC. Shields provide additional AC bonus.

See also: help inventory, help ac
wear:
Wear / Equip Command

Usage: wear <item>
       equip <item>

Equip an item from your inventory. Items go to the appropriate
slot (weapons to main_hand, armor to body, etc.).

See also: help remove, help equipment
remove:
Remove / Unequip Command

Usage: remove <item>
       unequip <item>

Unequip an item and return it to your inventory.

See also: help wear, help equipment
inventory:
Inventory Command

Usage: inventory
       inv

Show all items you are carrying, their weight, and your
encumbrance level (Light/Medium/Heavy/Overloaded).

Encumbrance limits (based on Strength):
  Light:  STR x 5 lbs
  Medium: STR x 10 lbs
  Heavy:  STR x 15 lbs

See also: help get, help drop, help equipment
get:
Get / Take Command

Usage: get <item>
       take <item>
       get <item> <container>

Pick up an item from the ground or from a container.

See also: help drop, help inventory, help loot, help put
drop:
Drop Command

Usage: drop <item>

Drop an item from your inventory onto the ground.

See also: help get, help inventory
score:
Score Command

Usage: score

Display your full character sheet including:
  Name, race, class, level, alignment, deity
  HP, AC, ability scores and modifiers
  Saves (Fort, Ref, Will)
  XP, gold, movement points
  Active conditions and buffs

See also: help stats, help conditions
stats:
Stats Command

Usage: stats

Toggle verbose stat display mode.

See also: help score
conditions:
Conditions Command

Usage: conditions
       status

Show all active conditions affecting your character and their
remaining duration (in rounds).

Common Conditions (D&D 3.5):
  Prone:      -4 melee attacks, -4 AC vs melee, +4 AC vs ranged
  Grappled:   Cannot move, -4 Dex, -2 attacks
  Stunned:    Cannot act, -2 AC, loses Dex to AC
  Disarmed:   -4 attacks, forced to fight unarmed
  Blinded:    -2 AC, loses Dex to AC, 50% miss chance
  Frightened: -2 attacks/saves/skills, must flee
  Poisoned:   Varies by poison

Conditions expire after their duration or can be cleared by
spells, resting, or respawning.

See also: help combat, help rest, help death
skills:
Skills Command

Usage: skills

List all your trained skills and their ranks.

Skill checks use: d20 + skill ranks + ability modifier + misc bonuses

Skill commands (type the skill name to attempt a check):
  balance, bluff, climb, concentration, diplomacy, disguise,
  escapeartist, gather, heal, hide, intimidate, jump, knowledge,
  listen, movesilently, openlock, perform, profession, ride,
  search, sensemotive, sleightofhand, spot, survival, swim,
  tumble, usemagic, userope

Class skills cost 1 point per rank. Cross-class cost 2 points.
Max class skill rank: level + 3
Max cross-class rank: (level + 3) / 2

See also: help score
cast:
Cast Command

Usage: cast <spell name> [target]

Cast a prepared/known spell. Deducts a spell slot of the
appropriate level.

Requirements:
  - Spell must be known or prepared
  - Must have an available spell slot
  - Must be able to speak (for Verbal components)
  - Must be able to move hands (for Somatic components)
  - Must not be silenced, paralyzed, or nauseated

Examples:
  cast magic missile goblin
  cast cure light wounds
  cast bless

In combat, use 'queue spell <name> [target]' to cast on your
next turn instead of auto-attacking.

See also: help spells, help prepare, help queue
spells:
Spells Command

Usage: spells

List all spells you know, organized by spell level.
Shows remaining spell slots per level.

See also: help cast, help prepare, help spellinfo, help domains
spellinfo:
Spell Info Command

Usage: spellinfo <spell name>

Show detailed information about a spell: school, level, components,
casting time, range, duration, saving throw, and description.

See also: help spells, help cast
prepare:
Prepare / Memorize Command

Usage: prepare <spell name>
       memorize <spell name>

Prepare a spell for casting (Wizards/Clerics). You must prepare
spells during rest. Prepared spells fill your spell slots.

See also: help spells, help cast, help rest
spellbook:
Spellbook Command

Usage: spellbook

View your spellbook showing all prepared and available spells.

See also: help spells, help prepare
domains:
Domains Command

Usage: domains

Show your Cleric domains, their granted powers, and domain spells.
Domains are chosen during character creation and grant bonus
spells and special abilities.

See also: help cast, help spells
look:
Look Command

Usage: look
       look <target>

Look at your surroundings. Shows the room description, NPCs,
other players, items on the ground, and corpses.

With a target, examines that mob or player.

See also: help movement, help exits
exits:
Exits Command

Usage: exits

Show all available exits from the current room with their
direction and destination name.

See also: help movement, help look
kill:
Kill Command

Usage: kill <target>

Attack a mob or player, initiating combat. Uses the D&D 3.5
combat system with initiative, attack rolls, and damage.

Combat is turn-based with auto-attack by default. Use 'queue'
to substitute special actions for your next auto-attack.

See also: help combat, help flee, help queue, help target
fullattack:
Full Attack Command

Usage: fullattack <target>
       fa <target>

Make a full attack (full-round action) against a target.
With high BAB, you get iterative attacks at -5 penalty each:
  BAB +6:  2 attacks (+6/+1)
  BAB +11: 3 attacks (+11/+6/+1)
  BAB +16: 4 attacks (+16/+11/+6/+1)

See also: help kill, help combat
powerattack:
Power Attack Command

Usage: powerattack <0-BAB>
       powerattack off

Trade attack bonus for damage bonus. Requires the Power Attack
feat. You can trade up to your BAB in attack for an equal
bonus to damage.

Example: powerattack 3  (trade -3 attack for +3 damage)

See also: help combat, help combatexpertise
combatexpertise:
Combat Expertise Command

Usage: combatexpertise <0-5>
       combatexpertise off

Trade attack bonus for AC bonus. Requires the Combat Expertise
feat. Trade up to 5 attack for equal AC bonus.

See also: help combat, help powerattack
disarm:
Disarm Command

Usage: disarm <target>

Attempt to knock a weapon from an opponent's hand (D&D 3.5 rules).

Mechanics:
  1. Melee touch attack to grab the weapon
  2. Opposed attack roll (BAB + Str + size)
  3. If you win, target is disarmed (-4 attacks, unarmed only)
  4. If you win by 10+, weapon flies 10 feet away
  5. If you lose, target can attempt to disarm you

Improved Disarm feat: +4 bonus, no attack of opportunity.

See also: help combat
trip:
Trip Command

Usage: trip <target>

Attempt to knock an opponent prone (D&D 3.5 rules).

Mechanics:
  1. Melee touch attack
  2. Opposed Str check (defender uses higher of Str/Dex)
  3. Size modifier applies (x4)
  4. If you lose, defender can counter-trip you

Prone effects: -4 melee attacks, -4 AC vs melee, +4 AC vs ranged.
Improved Trip feat: +4 bonus, no AoO, free attack on success.

See also: help combat, help conditions
bullrush:
Bull Rush Command

Usage: bullrush <target>

Attempt to push an opponent back (D&D 3.5 rules).

Mechanics:
  1. Move into defender's space (provokes AoO)
  2. Opposed Str check with size modifiers
  3. If you win: push back 5 ft + 5 per 5 margin
  4. If you lose: you are pushed back 5 ft

Improved Bull Rush feat: +4 bonus, no AoO.

See also: help combat
grapple:
Grapple Command

Usage: grapple <target>

Attempt to grab and restrain an opponent (D&D 3.5 rules).

Mechanics:
  1. Melee touch attack (provokes AoO)
  2. Opposed grapple check (BAB + Str + size)
  3. If you win: both combatants are grappled

Grappled: Cannot move, -4 Dex, -2 attacks, light weapons only.

While grappling, use:
  gdamage <target>  - Deal damage
  gpin <target>     - Attempt to pin
  gescape           - Attempt to escape

Improved Grapple feat: +4 bonus, no AoO.

See also: help combat, help conditions
overrun:
Overrun Command

Usage: overrun <target>

Attempt to move through an opponent's space (D&D 3.5 rules).

Mechanics:
  1. Defender can choose to step aside or block
  2. If block: opposed Str check with size modifiers
  3. If you win: defender knocked prone
  4. If defender wins by 5+: you are knocked prone

Improved Overrun feat: +4 bonus, defender cannot avoid.

See also: help combat
sunder:
Sunder Command

Usage: sunder <target>

Attempt to damage an opponent's weapon (D&D 3.5 rules).

Mechanics:
  1. Provokes AoO from defender
  2. Opposed attack roll
  3. If you win: deal damage to target's weapon

Improved Sunder feat: +4 bonus, no AoO.

See also: help combat
feint:
Feint Command

Usage: feint <target>

Attempt to bluff an opponent to deny their Dex bonus to AC.

Mechanics:
  Opposed check: your Bluff + Cha vs target's Sense Motive + Wis
  If you win: target loses Dex to AC for your next attack

Improved Feint feat: feint as a move action instead of standard.

See also: help combat
whirlwind:
Whirlwind Attack Command

Usage: whirlwind

Attack all adjacent enemies at once (full-round action).
One attack at highest BAB against each enemy.

Requires: Whirlwind Attack feat (prereqs: Dex 13, Int 13,
Combat Expertise, Dodge, Mobility, Spring Attack, BAB +4)

See also: help combat
springattack:
Spring Attack Command

Usage: springattack <target>

Move, attack, and continue moving. Target does not get an
attack of opportunity for this attack.

Requires: Spring Attack feat (prereqs: Dex 13, Dodge, Mobility, BAB +4)

See also: help combat
stunningfist:
Stunning Fist Command

Usage: stunningfist <target>
       stun <target>

Attempt to stun an opponent with an unarmed strike.

Mechanics:
  1. Make a normal unarmed attack
  2. If it hits: target makes Fort save (DC 10 + level/2 + Wis mod)
  3. If save fails: target stunned for 1 round

Stunned: Cannot act, -2 AC, loses Dex to AC.
Requires: Stunning Fist feat.

See also: help combat, help conditions
gdamage:
Grapple Damage Command

Usage: gdamage <target>

Deal unarmed or light weapon damage to a grappled opponent.
Both you and the target must have the grappled condition.

See also: help grapple
gpin:
Grapple Pin Command

Usage: gpin <target>

Attempt to pin a grappled opponent with an opposed grapple check.
Pinned targets lose Dex bonus to AC and take -4 AC vs others.

See also: help grapple
gescape:
Grapple Escape Command

Usage: gescape

Attempt to escape from a grapple using an opposed grapple check
or Escape Artist skill check.

See also: help grapple
talk:
Talk / Ask / Converse Command

Usage: talk <npc name> [message]
       ask <npc name> [message]
       converse <npc name> [message]

Speak to an NPC in the room. NPCs respond with AI-generated
roleplay dialogue based on their personality and knowledge.

With no message, sends a greeting.
Type 'talk' alone to see who you can talk to.

Examples:
  talk mira hello
  talk guard what is this place?
  ask merchant what do you sell?

See also: help shops
quest:
Quest Command

Usage: quest

Show your current quest log with objectives and progress.
Quest types:
  KILL:    Defeat specific mobs
  DELIVER: Give an item to an NPC
  EXPLORE: Visit specific rooms

Quests are assigned by NPCs. Complete objectives to earn rewards.

See also: help give
levelup:
Level Up Command

Usage: levelup

Advance to the next level when you have enough XP.

XP Requirements (cumulative):
  Level 2:  1,000 XP     Level 11: 55,000 XP
  Level 3:  3,000 XP     Level 12: 66,000 XP
  Level 4:  6,000 XP     Level 13: 78,000 XP
  Level 5:  10,000 XP    Level 14: 91,000 XP
  Level 6:  15,000 XP    Level 15: 105,000 XP
  Level 7:  21,000 XP    Level 16: 120,000 XP
  Level 8:  28,000 XP    Level 17: 136,000 XP
  Level 9:  36,000 XP    Level 18: 153,000 XP
  Level 10: 45,000 XP    Level 19: 171,000 XP
                         Level 20: 190,000 XP

On level up you gain:
  - HP increase (class hit die + CON mod)
  - Skill points (class base + INT mod)
  - Feat at levels 1, 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18
  - Ability score increase at levels 4, 8, 12, 16, 20
  - New spell slots and spells known

See also: help progression, help score
progression:
Progression Command

Usage: progression

Display the D&D 3.5 character advancement chart showing feat levels,
ability score increases, and skill point totals by level.

See also: help levelup, help skills, help score
recall:
Recall Command

Usage: recall

Teleport instantly to the Central Aetherial Altar (starting room).
Cannot be used during combat.

See also: help movement, help respawn
who:
Who Command

Usage: who

List all players currently online, showing their name, level,
race, and class.

See also: help chat
time:
Time Command

Usage: time

Show the current in-game date and time. Oreka uses an accelerated
cycle. Time of day affects room lighting, NPC schedules, and atmosphere.

See also: help weather, help look, help survival
companion:
Companion Command

Usage: companion

View your current animal companions or familiars.
Companion recruitment and management is coming in a future update.

See also: help score
ac:
Armor Class (AC)

Your AC determines how hard you are to hit in combat.

AC = 10 + Armor Bonus + Shield Bonus + Dex Modifier
     + Size Modifier + Deflection + Natural Armor + Dodge

Touch AC:      Ignores armor and shield bonuses
Flat-Footed AC: Ignores Dex and Dodge bonuses

Heavy armor limits your maximum Dex bonus to AC.
Use 'wear' to equip armor and 'score' to see your AC.

See also: help equipment, help score
xp:
Experience Points (XP)

XP is earned by defeating monsters. The amount depends on
the level difference between you and the mob's CR:

  You vs CR  |  XP Award
  -----------|----------
  CR-4       |  75 XP
  CR-3       |  100 XP
  CR-2       |  150 XP
  CR-1       |  200 XP
  Equal CR   |  300 XP
  CR+1       |  450 XP
  CR+2       |  600 XP
  CR+3       |  900 XP
  CR+4       |  1,200 XP

Use 'levelup' when you have enough XP.
Dying costs 10% of your current XP.

See also: help levelup, help death
components:
Components Command

Usage: components

Show all raw materials, spell components, and reagents
available in the game world.

See also: help cast, help spells
say:
Say Command

Usage: say <message>

Speak to everyone in the room. Other players in the room
will see your message.

See also: help chat
tell:
Tell / Whisper Command

Usage: tell <player> <message>
       whisper <player> <message>

Send a private message to another player anywhere in the world.
Use 'reply' to respond to the last tell you received.

See also: help chat, help reply
reply:
Reply Command

Usage: reply <message>

Reply to the last person who sent you a tell.

See also: help tell, help chat
emote:
Emote Command

Usage: emote <action>
       me <action>

Perform a roleplay action visible to everyone in the room.

Examples:
  emote waves hello
  me draws their sword

See also: help chat
global:
Global / Chat Command

Usage: global <message>
       chat <message>

Send a message to all players in the world.

See also: help chat
shout:
Shout / Yell Command

Usage: shout <message>
       yell <message>

Broadcast a message to nearby areas.

See also: help chat
ooc:
OOC Command

Usage: ooc <message>

Send an out-of-character message to everyone in the room.

See also: help chat
sense:
Kin-Sense Command

Usage: sense
       sense <target>
       kinsense

Actively focus your Kin-sense to read the resonance in the room.
Without arguments, gives a summary of all presences. With a target
name, gives a detailed reading of that specific entity.

Kin-sense is automatic on room entry and when using 'look'.

Resonance Categories:
  Harmonic (Kin) - Warm presence; race and element readable
  Wild Static    - Alive but not Kin (animals, Tanuki, fey)
  Breach Static  - Alien, unsettling (Goblins, Wargs, Orcs, etc.)
  Null           - A wound in the harmony (Silentborn, Severed)
  Void           - Predatory silence (Domnathar)
  No Registration- Simply absent (Undead, Constructs, Farborn)
  Raw Static     - Overwhelming elemental force (pure elementals)

Racial Bonuses:
  Half-Giant: +4  Elf: +2  Dwarf: +2  Halfling: +1  Human: +0

Kin-sense is NOT magic. It works in anti-magic zones.
It IS suppressed by False Silence and worked Domnathar metal.

See also: help death, help conditions
kinsense:
Kin-Sense Command

Usage: sense
       sense <target>
       kinsense

Actively focus your Kin-sense to read the resonance in the room.
Without arguments, gives a summary of all presences. With a target
name, gives a detailed reading of that specific entity.

Kin-sense is automatic on room entry and when using 'look'.

Resonance Categories:
  Harmonic (Kin) - Warm presence; race and element readable
  Wild Static    - Alive but not Kin (animals, Tanuki, fey)
  Breach Static  - Alien, unsettling (Goblins, Wargs, Orcs, etc.)
  Null           - A wound in the harmony (Silentborn, Severed)
  Void           - Predatory silence (Domnathar)
  No Registration- Simply absent (Undead, Constructs, Farborn)
  Raw Static     - Overwhelming elemental force (pure elementals)

Racial Bonuses:
  Half-Giant: +4  Elf: +2  Dwarf: +2  Halfling: +1  Human: +0

Kin-sense is NOT magic. It works in anti-magic zones.
It IS suppressed by False Silence and worked Domnathar metal.

See also: help death, help conditions
balance:
Balance Skill

Usage: balance

Attempt a Dexterity-based Balance skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to walk on narrow ledges, slippery surfaces, or unstable ground.

Ability: Dexterity
Trained only: No

This command also works at a banker to check your account balance.
At a bank: balance shows your current gold on deposit.

See also: help skills, help climb, help tumble
bluff:
Bluff Skill

Usage: bluff

Attempt a Charisma-based Bluff skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to lie convincingly, feint in combat, create a diversion,
or pass secret messages through hidden meaning.

Ability: Charisma
Trained only: No

In combat, use 'feint <target>' to use Bluff to deny a foe's Dex to AC.

See also: help skills, help feint, help diplomacy, help intimidate
climb:
Climb Skill

Usage: climb

Attempt a Strength-based Climb skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to scale walls, cliff faces, ropes, or other vertical surfaces.

Ability: Strength
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help jump, help swim
concentration:
Concentration Skill

Usage: concentration

Attempt a Constitution-based Concentration skill check (D&D 3.5).
Spellcasters use Concentration to maintain spells while taking damage,
being grappled, or casting in adverse conditions.

Ability: Constitution
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help cast, help spellcraft
decipher:
Decipher Script Skill

Usage: decipher

Attempt an Intelligence-based Decipher Script check (D&D 3.5).
Used to read ancient texts, coded messages, or unfamiliar writing.

Ability: Intelligence
Trained only: Yes

See also: help skills, help spellcraft, help knowledge
diplomacy:
Diplomacy Skill

Usage: diplomacy

Attempt a Charisma-based Diplomacy skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to improve NPC attitudes, negotiate, and persuade.

Ability: Charisma
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help bluff, help intimidate, help sensemotive
disable:
Disable Device Skill

Usage: disable

Attempt an Intelligence-based Disable Device check (D&D 3.5).
Used to disarm traps, sabotage mechanical devices, or deactivate locks.

Ability: Intelligence
Trained only: Yes

See also: help skills, help disarmtrap, help openlock, help search
disguise:
Disguise Skill

Usage: disguise

Attempt a Charisma-based Disguise skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to change your appearance to impersonate another person or creature.

Ability: Charisma
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help bluff, help hide
escape:
Escape Artist Skill

Usage: escape

Attempt a Dexterity-based Escape Artist check (D&D 3.5).
Used to slip free from ropes, nets, grapples, or tight spaces.

Ability: Dexterity
Trained only: No

In combat, use 'gescape' to attempt to break out of a grapple.

See also: help skills, help grapple, help gescape
forgery:
Forgery Skill

Usage: forgery

Attempt an Intelligence-based Forgery skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to create or detect fake documents, seals, and written materials.

Ability: Intelligence
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help decipher, help bluff
handle:
Handle Animal Skill

Usage: handle

Attempt a Charisma-based Handle Animal skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to command, train, or calm animals and magical beasts.

Ability: Charisma
Trained only: Yes

See also: help skills, help ride, help companion
heal:
Heal Skill

Usage: heal

Attempt a Wisdom-based Heal skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to provide first aid, long-term care, treat poison, or treat disease.

Ability: Wisdom
Trained only: No

Note: This uses the Heal skill. For magical healing, use 'cast cure light wounds' etc.

See also: help skills, help cast, help rest
intimidate:
Intimidate Skill

Usage: intimidate

Attempt a Charisma-based Intimidate skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to demoralize opponents in combat or coerce NPCs outside combat.

Ability: Charisma
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help bluff, help diplomacy
jump:
Jump Skill

Usage: jump

Attempt a Strength-based Jump skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to leap across gaps, over obstacles, or up to grab ledges.

Ability: Strength
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help climb, help tumble
knowledge:
Knowledge Skill

Usage: knowledge [type]

Attempt an Intelligence-based Knowledge skill check (D&D 3.5).
Knowledge is actually a group of skills, each covering a different field:
  arcana, nature, history, religion, dungeoneering, planes, etc.

Ability: Intelligence
Trained only: Yes

Examples:
  knowledge arcana
  knowledge nature
  knowledge religion

See also: help skills, help spellcraft, help decipher
listen:
Listen Skill

Usage: listen

Attempt a Wisdom-based Listen skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to hear approaching creatures, overhear conversations, or detect
invisible opponents by sound.

Ability: Wisdom
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help spot, help search, help scan
movesilently:
Move Silently Skill

Usage: movesilently

Attempt a Dexterity-based Move Silently check (D&D 3.5).
Used to move without making sound. Contested by opponent's Listen.
Use 'sneak' to toggle automatic silent movement.

Ability: Dexterity
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help sneak, help hide, help listen
openlock:
Open Lock Skill

Usage: openlock

Attempt a Dexterity-based Open Lock skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to pick locks without a key. See also the 'pick' command for
interacting with locked doors and containers.

Ability: Dexterity
Trained only: Yes

See also: help skills, help pick, help disable, help search
perform:
Perform Skill

Usage: perform

Attempt a Charisma-based Perform skill check (D&D 3.5).
Bards use Perform as the basis for bardic music abilities (inspire, etc.).
Covers singing, dancing, acting, playing instruments, and other arts.

Ability: Charisma
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help inspire
profession:
Profession Skill

Usage: profession

Attempt a Wisdom-based Profession skill check (D&D 3.5).
Represents a character's knowledge of a specific trade or craft.

Ability: Wisdom
Trained only: Yes

See also: help skills, help craft, help recipes
ride:
Ride Skill

Usage: ride

Attempt a Dexterity-based Ride skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to handle mounts in difficult situations, guide with knees,
or perform mounted combat maneuvers.

Ability: Dexterity
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help mount, help dismount, help handle
sensemotive:
Sense Motive Skill

Usage: sensemotive

Attempt a Wisdom-based Sense Motive check (D&D 3.5).
Used to detect lies, gauge moods, or resist feints in combat.
Contested by an opponent's Bluff skill.

Ability: Wisdom
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help bluff, help feint, help diplomacy
sleight:
Sleight of Hand Skill

Usage: sleight

Attempt a Dexterity-based Sleight of Hand check (D&D 3.5).
Used to palm small objects, pick pockets, or conceal items.
Contested by observer's Spot checks.

Ability: Dexterity
Trained only: Yes

See also: help skills, help hide, help disguise
spellcraft:
Spellcraft Skill

Usage: spellcraft

Attempt an Intelligence-based Spellcraft check (D&D 3.5).
Used to identify spells being cast, understand magical writings,
and learn new spells from scrolls.

Ability: Intelligence
Trained only: Yes

See also: help skills, help concentration, help knowledge, help identify
spot:
Spot Skill

Usage: spot

Attempt a Wisdom-based Spot skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to notice hidden creatures, objects, or details.
Contested by Hide checks for hidden opponents.

Ability: Wisdom
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help listen, help search, help scan
survival:
Survival Skill

Usage: survival

Attempt a Wisdom-based Survival skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to track creatures, forage for food, endure harsh weather,
and navigate wilderness environments.

Ability: Wisdom
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help track, help gather, help weather
swim:
Swim Skill

Usage: swim

Attempt a Strength-based Swim skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to move through water, resist currents, or avoid drowning.

Ability: Strength
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help holdbreath, help climb
tumble:
Tumble Skill

Usage: tumble

Attempt a Dexterity-based Tumble skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to fall safely, move past opponents without provoking attacks
of opportunity, or soften the impact of a fall.

Ability: Dexterity
Trained only: Yes

See also: help skills, help jump, help balance, help escape
usemagic:
Use Magic Device Skill

Usage: usemagic

Attempt a Charisma-based Use Magic Device check (D&D 3.5).
Used to activate magic items whose use is normally restricted,
such as wands or scrolls outside your class.

Ability: Charisma
Trained only: Yes

See also: help skills, help quaff, help read, help use
userope:
Use Rope Skill

Usage: userope

Attempt a Dexterity-based Use Rope skill check (D&D 3.5).
Used to tie secure knots, bind prisoners, or set up rope systems.

Ability: Dexterity
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help escape, help climb
gatherinfo:
Gather Information Skill

Usage: gatherinfo

Attempt a Charisma-based Gather Information check (D&D 3.5).
Used to ask around town for rumors, news, or specific information
about people and places. Takes time and may cost money.

Ability: Charisma
Trained only: No

See also: help skills, help diplomacy, help bluff
take10:
Take 10 Command

Usage: take10 <skill name>

Attempt a skill check as if you rolled a 10 on the d20 (D&D 3.5).
Taking 10 is allowed when you are not rushed or threatened.
You cannot take 10 in combat or under stress.

Examples:
  take10 climb
  take10 swim
  take10 balance

See also: help take20, help check, help skills
take20:
Take 20 Command

Usage: take20 <skill name>

Attempt a skill check as if you rolled a 20 on the d20 (D&D 3.5).
Taking 20 represents trying repeatedly until you succeed.
Requires extra time and cannot be used if failure has consequences.
You cannot take 20 in combat.

Examples:
  take20 open lock
  take20 search

See also: help take10, help check, help skills
skilllist:
Skill List Command

Usage: skilllist

Display a complete list of all D&D 3.5 skills, their governing
ability score, and whether they are trained-only.

Use 'skills' to see your own trained skills and ranks.
Use 'check <skill>' to attempt a skill check by name.

See also: help skills, help check, help take10, help take20
helpfeats:
Help Feats Command

Usage: helpfeats [feat name]

Browse all feats in the game system. Without an argument, lists all
feats, their type (passive or active), and brief descriptions.
With a feat name, shows full details and usage instructions.

Examples:
  helpfeats
  helpfeats power attack
  helpfeats improved disarm

See also: help feats, help levelup, help progression
questlog:
Quest Log Command

Usage: questlog [active|complete|all]

View your quest journal. By default shows active quests.
  active   - Show only quests in progress
  complete - Show only completed quests
  all      - Show all quests

Each entry shows: quest name, objectives, progress, and reward.

See also: help quest, help give
talkto:
Talkto Command

Usage: talkto <npc name>

Initiate dialogue with an NPC in the room.
Unlike 'talk', 'talkto' targets a specific NPC by name for extended
conversation, triggering their greeting and available dialogue topics.

Examples:
  talkto merchant
  talkto guard captain

See also: help talk, help quest
@backup:
Admin: Backup Player

Usage: @backup <player name>

Create a backup snapshot of a player's saved data file.
Requires immortal (admin) privileges.

See also: help @restore, help @save
@restore:
Admin: Restore Player

Usage: @restore <player name> [timestamp]

Restore a player's data from a previously created backup.
If a timestamp is given, restores that specific backup version.
Requires immortal (admin) privileges.

See also: help @backup, help @save
@save:
Admin: Save Player

Usage: @save <player name>

Force-save a player's current data to disk immediately.
Normally players are saved automatically; this forces an immediate write.
Requires immortal (admin) privileges.

See also: help @backup, help @restore
charge:
Charge Command

Usage: charge <target>

Make a charge attack (D&D 3.5 full-round action).
You must move at least 10 feet in a straight line before attacking.

Effects:
  +2 attack roll bonus
  -2 AC penalty until your next turn

See also: help combat, help fullattack
totaldefense:
Total Defense Command

Usage: totaldefense
       td

Focus entirely on defense for one round (D&D 3.5 standard action).
Grants +4 dodge bonus to AC but you cannot attack.

See also: help fightdef, help combat, help ac
fightdef:
Fighting Defensively Command

Usage: fightdef
       defensive

Fight defensively for one round (D&D 3.5).
Take -4 penalty to all attacks in exchange for +2 dodge bonus to AC.
With Combat Expertise feat: trade up to -5 attack for up to +5 AC.

See also: help totaldefense, help combat, help combatexpertise
flurry:
Flurry of Blows Command

Usage: flurry <target>

Monk ability: make an extra attack at -2 penalty to all attacks.
At higher monk levels, the penalty decreases and additional attacks
are gained. Requires unarmed or monk weapons only.

See also: help combat, help stunningfist
smite:
Smite Evil / Good Command

Usage: smite <target>

Paladin / Blackguard ability: add Charisma modifier to attack roll
and class level to damage against an evil (or good) target.
Usable once per day (more at higher levels).

See also: help combat, help layonhands, help turn
rage:
Rage Command

Usage: rage

Barbarian ability: enter a battle rage for several rounds.
While raging:
  +4 Strength, +4 Constitution, +2 Will saves
  -2 AC penalty
  Cannot use skills that require calm (Diplomacy, etc.)

After rage ends: fatigued for twice the rage duration.

See also: help combat, help conditions
inspire:
Inspire Courage / Bardic Music Command

Usage: inspire

Bard ability: use bardic music to inspire allies.
Granted effects depend on the bardic performance used:
  Inspire Courage: +1 morale to attacks, damage, and fear saves

Requires Perform skill ranks.

See also: help perform, help skills
turn:
Turn Undead Command

Usage: turn [target]
       turnundead [target]

Cleric / Paladin ability: channel positive energy to turn, rebuke,
or destroy undead. Each use expends one turn attempt per day.

Effect depends on undead HD vs. your turning check:
  Turn: undead flee for 10 rounds
  Destroy: undead with weak enough HD are destroyed outright

See also: help combat, help smite, help layonhands
wildshape:
Wild Shape Command

Usage: wildshape [form]

Druid ability: transform into an animal form.
Granted HP, Str, Dex, Con, and natural armor of the animal form.
Usable multiple times per day depending on druid level.

See also: help combat, help score
favoredenemy:
Favored Enemy Command

Usage: favoredenemy
       fe

Ranger ability: display your favored enemy bonuses.
You gain +2 (or more) to attack rolls, damage, and skill checks
(Bluff, Listen, Sense Motive, Spot, Survival) against favored enemies.

Favored enemies are chosen at levels 1, 5, 10, 15, and 20.

See also: help combat, help skills, help track
layonhands:
Lay On Hands Command

Usage: layonhands [target]
       loh [target]

Paladin ability: heal hit points by touch.
Restores CHA modifier x paladin level HP per day (pool shared).
With no target, heals yourself.

See also: help smite, help turn, help combat
summon:
Summon Command

Usage: summon <creature type>

Summon a creature to fight alongside you.
Clerics and Druids can summon nature allies or monsters.
Summoned creatures last for a limited duration.

See also: help familiar, help spells, help cast
familiar:
Familiar Command

Usage: familiar

Display or manage your familiar (Sorcerer / Wizard ability).
Familiars provide special bonuses based on their type and grant
their master shared senses at higher levels.

See also: help companion, help summon
dispel:
Dispel Command

Usage: dispel <target>
       cast dispel magic <target>

Attempt to dispel active magical effects on a target.
Makes a caster level check (d20 + caster level) against each effect.
Dispel magic affects one spell; greater dispel magic can affect several.

See also: help cast, help spells, help affects
north:
Movement Commands

Usage: north / south / east / west / up / down
       n / s / e / w / u / d

Move in the specified direction if an exit exists and is passable.
Doors must be open before you can pass through them.

See also: help open, help close, help exits, help recall, help speedwalk
south:
Movement Commands

Usage: north / south / east / west / up / down
       n / s / e / w / u / d

Move in the specified direction if an exit exists and is passable.

See also: help open, help close, help exits, help recall, help speedwalk
east:
Movement Commands

Usage: north / south / east / west / up / down
       n / s / e / w / u / d

Move in the specified direction if an exit exists and is passable.

See also: help open, help close, help exits, help recall, help speedwalk
west:
Movement Commands

Usage: north / south / east / west / up / down
       n / s / e / w / u / d

Move in the specified direction if an exit exists and is passable.

See also: help open, help close, help exits, help recall, help speedwalk
up:
Movement Commands

Usage: north / south / east / west / up / down
       n / s / e / w / u / d

Move in the specified direction if an exit exists and is passable.

See also: help open, help close, help exits, help recall, help speedwalk
down:
Movement Commands

Usage: north / south / east / west / up / down
       n / s / e / w / u / d

Move in the specified direction if an exit exists and is passable.

See also: help open, help close, help exits, help recall, help speedwalk
open:
Open Command

Usage: open <door direction or name>

Open a closed door or container in the room.
Doors must be unlocked before they can be opened.

See also: help close, help lock, help unlock, help pick
close:
Close Command

Usage: close <door direction or name>

Close an open door in the room.

See also: help open, help lock, help unlock
lock:
Lock Command

Usage: lock <door direction or name>

Lock a closed door. You must have the appropriate key.

See also: help unlock, help open, help pick
unlock:
Unlock Command

Usage: unlock <door direction or name>

Unlock a locked door. You must have the appropriate key.

See also: help lock, help open, help pick
pick:
Pick Lock Command

Usage: pick <door direction or name>

Attempt to pick a lock without the key using the Open Lock skill.
Difficulty depends on the lock's quality.

See also: help unlock, help openlock, help disable
speedwalk:
Speedwalk Command

Usage: speedwalk <directions>
       sw <directions>
       walk <directions>

Execute a sequence of movement commands with one command.
Use letters: n (north), s (south), e (east), w (west), u (up), d (down).

Examples:
  speedwalk nnnwe
  sw 3n2w

See also: help movement, help recall
fly:
Fly Command

Usage: fly

Begin flying if you have a natural fly speed or an active fly spell.
Flying allows movement over hazards and access to aerial exits.
Use 'land' to return to the ground.

See also: help land, help movement
land:
Land Command

Usage: land

Land from flight. Use when you are flying and wish to return to ground.

See also: help fly, help movement
mount:
Mount Command

Usage: mount <animal name>

Mount a rideable animal or creature in the room.
Mounted movement uses the mount's speed. Requires Ride skill.

See also: help dismount, help ride
dismount:
Dismount Command

Usage: dismount

Dismount from your current mount, returning to foot travel.

See also: help mount, help ride
sit:
Sit Command

Usage: sit

Sit down to rest. You are less mobile while sitting.
You cannot move or fight effectively while seated.

See also: help stand, help kneel, help rest, help wake
stand:
Stand Command

Usage: stand

Stand up from a seated, kneeling, or prone position.

See also: help sit, help kneel, help wake
kneel:
Kneel Command

Usage: kneel

Kneel down. Useful for roleplay at shrines, before NPCs, etc.
You cannot move effectively while kneeling.

See also: help sit, help stand, help wake
wake:
Wake Command

Usage: wake

Wake up from sleep or unconsciousness (if able).

See also: help rest, help sit, help stand
group:
Group Command

Usage: group
       group <player name>

View or manage your group. Groups allow XP sharing and coordinated
combat with other players.
  group            - Show group status
  group <name>     - Invite or accept a player into your group

See also: help follow, help unfollow, help assist, help gtell
follow:
Follow Command

Usage: follow <player name>

Begin following another player, automatically moving when they move.
You must be in the same room to start following.

See also: help unfollow, help group, help assist
unfollow:
Unfollow Command

Usage: unfollow

Stop following another player.

See also: help follow, help group
assist:
Assist Command

Usage: assist <player name>

Attack the same target that the specified player is fighting.
Useful in group combat to focus fire on one enemy.

See also: help group, help follow, help rescue
rescue:
Rescue Command

Usage: rescue <player name>

Step in to protect an ally in combat. You take over as the mob's
primary target, drawing its attacks away from your ally.

See also: help group, help assist, help combat
guild:
Guild / Clan Command

Usage: guild
       guild info
       guild join <name>
       guild leave
       guild roster
       guild deposit <amount>
       guild withdraw <amount>

Guilds are player organizations that share a bank, hall, and identity.
Use 'guild' with no args to see your current guild status.

See also: help clan, help group
mail:
Mail Command

Usage: mail
       sendmail <player> <message>
       readmail

The in-game mail system for player-to-player messages.
  mail             - Show your mailbox summary
  sendmail <name>  - Send a message to another player
  readmail         - Read messages in your mailbox

See also: help sendmail, help readmail, help board
sendmail:
Send Mail Command

Usage: sendmail <player name> <message>

Send an in-game mail message to another player.

See also: help mail, help readmail
readmail:
Read Mail Command

Usage: readmail
       readmail <message number>

Read messages in your mailbox. Without a number, lists all messages.
With a number, reads that specific message.

See also: help mail, help sendmail
board:
Bulletin Board Command

Usage: board
       post <message>
       readnote <number>

The in-game public bulletin board system.
  board     - List posts on the current area's board
  post      - Write a new post
  readnote  - Read a specific post

See also: help post, help readnote, help mail
post:
Post Command

Usage: post <subject>

Post a new message to the bulletin board in the current room.

See also: help board, help readnote
readnote:
Read Note Command

Usage: readnote <number>

Read a specific note on the bulletin board in the room.

See also: help board, help post
duel:
Duel Command

Usage: duel <player name>
       challenge <player name>

Challenge another player to a formal PvP duel.
Duels are consensual -- both players must agree before combat starts.
The loser is left at 1 HP and cannot be killed.

See also: help combat, help pvp
ignore:
Ignore Command

Usage: ignore
       ignore <player name>

Manage your ignore list.
  ignore           - Show your current ignore list
  ignore <name>    - Toggle ignore on/off for that player

Ignored players' tells, emotes, and chat messages are hidden from you.

See also: help tell, help ooc, help chat
afk:
AFK Command

Usage: afk
       afk <message>

Toggle your AFK (Away From Keyboard) status.
  afk            - Toggle AFK on or off
  afk <message>  - Go AFK with a custom away message

Players who send you a tell while AFK will see your message.

See also: help tell, help chat
title:
Title Command

Usage: title <new title>

Set a custom title that appears after your name in 'who' and 'look'.

See also: help who, help score
put:
Put Command

Usage: put <item> <container>

Place an item from your inventory into a container in the room
or in your inventory.

See also: help get, help peek, help inventory
peek:
Peek Command

Usage: peek <container>

Look inside a container without taking anything.

See also: help put, help get, help inventory
quaff:
Quaff / Drink Command

Usage: quaff <potion name>
       drink <potion name>

Drink a potion from your inventory, activating its magical effect.

See also: help use, help read, help inventory
use:
Use Command

Usage: use <item name> [target]

Activate a wand, staff, or other usable magic item.
For potions use 'quaff'; for scrolls use 'read'.

See also: help quaff, help read, help identify
light:
Light Command

Usage: light <item name>

Light a torch, lantern, or other light source in your inventory.
Light sources illuminate dark rooms, affecting descriptions and combat.

See also: help extinguish, help look
extinguish:
Extinguish Command

Usage: extinguish <item name>

Put out a lit light source.

See also: help light, help look
eat:
Eat Command

Usage: eat <food item>

Eat a food item from your inventory to reduce hunger.
Hunger and thirst affect HP recovery when resting.

See also: help drink_water, help rest, help survivalmode
drink_water:
Drink Water Command

Usage: drink_water

Drink water to quench thirst. You need a water source nearby
or a water container in your inventory.

See also: help eat, help rest, help survivalmode
fish:
Fish Command

Usage: fish

Attempt to catch fish in a body of water. Requires being in a room
with a water source. Uses Survival skill.

See also: help gather, help mine, help survival
mine:
Mine Command

Usage: mine

Attempt to mine ore or gems from a rocky surface. Requires being
in a mine or rocky room. Uses Survival or craft skill.

See also: help gather, help fish, help craft, help recipes
gather:
Gather Command

Usage: gather

Gather herbs and natural materials from the environment.
Requires being in a forest, garden, or field room.
Uses Survival (DC 8). Has a 60-second cooldown.

See also: help fish, help mine, help survival
alias:
Alias System

Usage: alias                    - List all your aliases
       alias <name> <expansion>  - Create an alias
       alias <name>              - Delete an alias

Aliases let you create shortcuts for commands or command sequences.
You can have up to 20 aliases.

Examples:
  alias h help
  alias kk kill

See also: help config, help prompt
config:
Config / Toggle Command

Usage: config
       config <setting> [value]
       toggle <setting>

View and change personal settings:
  autoloot    - Automatically loot corpses
  autogold    - Automatically pick up gold
  autosac     - Automatically sacrifice worthless corpses
  autoattack  - Automatically attack each round
  wimpy       - HP threshold to auto-flee
  survivalmode - Track hunger and thirst

See also: help autoloot, help autogold, help wimpy, help autoattack
prompt:
Prompt Command

Usage: prompt
       prompt <format string>

Customize your command prompt. Variables:
  %h  - Current HP
  %H  - Max HP
  %m  - Current mana/spell points
  %M  - Max mana
  %v  - Current movement
  %V  - Max movement
  %x  - XP
  %g  - Gold
  %l  - Level
  %c  - Class

See also: help config, help score
autoloot:
Autoloot Command

Usage: autoloot [on|off]

Toggle automatic looting of corpses after combat.
When enabled, you automatically loot any corpse in the room after killing.

See also: help autogold, help autosac, help config, help loot
autogold:
Autogold Command

Usage: autogold [on|off]

Toggle automatic collection of gold from corpses and the ground.

See also: help autoloot, help autosac, help config
autosac:
Autosac Command

Usage: autosac [on|off]

Toggle automatic sacrifice of empty corpses. When a corpse has been
looted clean, it is automatically sacrificed for a small amount of XP.

See also: help autoloot, help autogold, help config
wimpy:
Wimpy Command

Usage: wimpy <hp value>
       wimpy 0

Set a HP threshold at which you will automatically flee combat.
Set to 0 to disable.

Example: wimpy 20   (flee when HP drops to 20 or below)

See also: help flee, help config, help combat
survivalmode:
Survival Mode Command

Usage: survivalmode [on|off]

Toggle tracking of hunger and thirst.
When enabled, you must eat and drink to maintain peak HP recovery.
When disabled, hunger and thirst have no effect.

See also: help eat, help drink_water, help rest, help config
deposit:
Deposit Command

Usage: deposit <amount>

Deposit gold into a bank account. You must be at a bank or financial
NPC to make deposits. Deposited gold is safe even after death.

See also: help withdraw, help balance, help gold
withdraw:
Withdraw Command

Usage: withdraw <amount>

Withdraw gold from your bank account. You must be at a bank or
financial NPC. You cannot withdraw more than your balance.

See also: help deposit, help balance, help gold
auction:
Auction Command

Usage: auction
       auction list
       auction bid <id> <amount>
       auction post <item name> <starting bid>
       auction cancel <id>

The player-run auction house. Post items for sale to all players.

See also: help buy, help sell, help worth
worth:
Worth Command

Usage: worth
       wealth

Show your total net worth: gold on hand, gold in the bank,
and estimated value of carried items and equipment.

Item condition affects combat effectiveness.

See also: help repairall, help craft, help equipment
craft:

Usage: craft <recipe name>

You must have the required materials in your inventory.

See also: help recipes, help gather, help mine, help fish
recipes:
Recipes Command

Usage: recipes
       recipes <category>

List all crafting recipes available to you.
Shows required materials and the resulting item.

See also: help craft, help gather, help mine
enchant:

Usage: enchant <item name> <enchantment>

Apply a magical enchantment to an item using reagents.
Enchantments add bonuses to weapons, armor, or accessories.
Requires specific materials and an Arcane caster level.

See also: help craft, help identify, help spellcraft
gamble:

Usage: gamble
       bet <amount>

Play a game of chance at a gambling establishment.
Bet gold and test your luck. You may win or lose gold.

See also: help gold, help auction
itemsets:
Item Sets Command

Usage: itemsets
       sets

Display all item set bonuses. Item sets are groups of equipment
that grant extra bonuses when multiple pieces are worn together.

See also: help equipment, help wear, help identify
examine:
Examine Command

Usage: examine <target>

Examine something in detail: a mob, item, piece of equipment,
or room feature. Shows stats, description, and condition.

During a Burnt Trail event, examine suspicious objects (floorboards,
vases, panels) to reveal hidden messages.
affects:
Affects / Buffs Command

Usage: affects
       buffs

your character, including their source and remaining duration.

compare:

Usage: compare <item1> <item2>

Compare the statistics of two items side by side.
Useful for deciding which piece of equipment to use.

See also: help identify, help equipment, help examine
consider:
Consider Command

Usage: consider <target>
       con <target>

Gives a qualitative assessment of the challenge.

identify:
Identify Command

Usage: identify <item name>
       id <item name>

Reveal the full magical properties of an item. You must be near a
sage, arcane shop, or cast 'identify' spell.

See also: help examine, help compare, help spellcraft
track:

Usage: track <creature name>

Attempt to track a creature using the Survival skill.
On success, shows the direction the creature went.

Ability: Survival (Wisdom-based)

See also: help survival, help scan, help search

Usage: weather

Check the current weather conditions in your zone.
Weather affects visibility (Spot/Listen), ranged combat, and travel.

See also: help time, help look, help survival
tnl:

Usage: tnl
       xp

leaderboard:

       top
       rankings

finger:

last login, and title.

areas:

       zones

List all known areas and zones in the world, with level ranges
and a brief description of each.

map:
Map Command

Usage: map

Display an ASCII map of your current area, showing rooms and exits
you have explored.

See also: help look, help exits, help areas
scan:
Scan Command

Usage: scan

Look into adjacent rooms in all directions for visible mobs and players.
Shows what is in the next room in each available exit direction.

where:

Usage: where
       where <mob or player name>

  where            - Show all players and their current areas
  where <name>     - Find a specific mob or player

achievements:

Usage: achievements
       achieve

skillcheck:
Skill Check Command

Usage: check <skill name> [dc]

Attempt a direct skill check. If a DC is given, reports success or failure.

  check climb 15
  check spot

reputation:
Reputation Command

Usage: reputation
       rep

Display your reputation standings with the various factions and
organizations in the world. Reputation affects NPC attitude,
shop prices, and available quests.

See also: help guild, help diplomacy, help score
remort:
Remort Command

Usage: remort

Once you have reached maximum level, remorting allows you to start again
at level 1 with bonus starting stats and a remort mark.
Each remort increases the challenge and the rewards.

See also: help levelup, help progression, help score
unbind:
Unbind / Free Command

Usage: unbind
       free

Free any captives bound in the current room. Captors must be
defeated first. The freed captive may hand you a token to
deliver to their family, share directions home, or depart on
their own — each captive reacts differently.

See also: present, journal, hidden
present:
Present Command

Usage: present <item>

Present a captive-token or faction item to an NPC in your room.
If the NPC recognizes the token (matching family representative),
they pay a reward in gold, items, and faction reputation.

Also works for Domnathar Cipher-Tablets — present to any faction
NPC who accepts intelligence for reputation credit.

See also: unbind, faction, journal
journal:
Journal Command

Usage: journal
       dossier

View your adventure journal showing rescues, faction standings,
exploration stats, combat kills, hidden quests, and your
character's 'known as' reputation tags.

See also: faction, hidden, score
faction:
Faction Command

Usage: faction            — Visual standings sheet
       faction info <name> — Detailed faction info
       faction join <name> — Join a faction
       faction leave       — Leave your faction
       factions / rep      — Aliases

Shows your reputation with all 12 factions as a visual bar from
Hated to Revered. Killing Domnathar-aligned mobs shifts your
reputation automatically.

See also: journal, reputation
hidden:
Hidden Quests Command

Usage: hidden

View hidden quests revealed to you by NPCs you've built enough
faction reputation with. Quests unlock at different rep thresholds
and are delivered in different ways (whispered, in private, by
appointment, or as direct orders).

See also: faction, journal
logroom:
Log Room Command

Usage: logroom            — Preview current room info
       logroom <notes>    — File a room-edit report

Report problems with the current room (description errors, missing
exits, etc.) for builders to review. Reports are saved per-room
in data/room_edits/.
